package: Goals 24-31 finite static-patch bridge theorem package
claim_boundary: finite mathematical-physics benchmark only; no continuum de Sitter, dS/CFT, or literal ER=EPR theorem is claimed
headline: screen shadows do not determine bridge algebra; cutoff-compatible strong continuity is a non-tautological finite gate preserving the quantum bridge split

major_claims.claim: Screen-visible cutoff data can match while bridge algebra differs.
The physical static-patch Schur kernel is CPTP/unital and preserves the bridge split.
A finite Hamiltonian/environment trace derives a random-unitary static-patch regulator.
A finite class of positive-definite Schur regulators preserves the no-go under bounded perturbations.
Static-patch-looking axioms select the regulator class only with vanishing cutoff continuity included.
KMS/detailed balance alone is insufficient; stationary modular twirling is a counterexample.
Short-time static-patch locality, fuzzy-sphere heat scaling, or shrinking Euclidean cap thickness imply approximate identity.
Identity-starting strong continuity plus delta_L Gamma_L -> 0 derives approximate identity and excludes stationary twirling/fixed-lapse controls.

frontier: derive cutoff-compatible strong continuity from continuum static-patch modular/thermal dynamics
derive the gate from a Euclidean cap or path-integral construction
study the Type-II/Type-III observer-algebra limit of the finite M_N versus C^N split
